name: Lead-Source ROI Tracker

parameters.content:
## Lead-Source ROI Tracker

### How it works

This workflow receives inbound lead events from Fluent Forms, CallRail, or any webhook-enabled form, normalizes the payload, and identifies the source channel using UTM parameters, gclid (Google Ads auto-tag), referrer, and keyword signals. Valid leads are tagged and created as contacts in Salesmate, logged to a Google Sheets Lead Log dashboard, optionally sent an acknowledgment email through the Bar-Compliance Guardrail, and always trigger a firm alert email with the full lead summary.

### Setup steps

- Create a Google Sheet named 'Lead Source ROI Dashboard' with a 'Lead Log' tab and the required column headers (see deploy guide).
- Set FIRM_NAME, FIRM_EMAIL, SALESMATE_BASE_URL, SALESMATE_LINK_NAME, ROI_SHEET_ID, FIRM_BOOKING_URL, GUARDRAIL_WORKFLOW_ID, and OPS1_SHEET_ID in n8n project Variables.
- Wire Salesmate API (Header Auth), Google Sheets OAuth2, and SMTP credentials in project → Credentials tab.
- Paste the Production URL into Fluent Forms and/or CallRail webhook settings.
- Deploy the Bar-Compliance Guardrail workflow first and set its ID in GUARDRAIL_WORKFLOW_ID.

### Customization

Extend the channel mapping in Tag Lead Source to recognize additional UTM sources specific to your ad campaigns. Add hidden UTM fields to every Fluent Form to improve attribution accuracy. Enable Google Ads auto-tagging to fill gclid automatically on every ad click.

## Receive and validate lead

Webhook intake and normalization. Accepts submissions from Fluent Forms, CallRail, or any JSON/form-encoded POST. Rejects developer test submissions and payloads with no identifiable contact.

## Tag and record lead

Maps source signals to a canonical channel name, creates the contact in Salesmate tagged with the channel, and logs the lead to your Google Sheets ROI dashboard.

## Acknowledge prospect

Sends the prospect a brief acknowledgment email (opt-out compliant) when an email address is available. Skips gracefully for phone-only leads like CallRail calls.

## Notify firm

Always emails your intake team the full lead summary — name, contact details, source channel, Salesmate link, and acknowledgment status — regardless of which path above was taken.

nodes.name: When Lead Submitted
nodes.notes:
Receives inbound lead events from any connected source — one URL handles all of them.

Paste the Production URL (visible after activating this workflow) into:
  Fluent Forms: Form Settings → Notifications → Webhooks → add this URL (format: JSON)
  CallRail: Settings → Integrations → Webhooks → All Call Events → add this URL
  Any web form: standard HTTP POST, JSON or form-encoded

The Normalize and Validate Lead step detects the source automatically and normalises everything into a consistent shape.

nodes.name: Normalize and Validate Lead
nodes.notes:
Reads and normalises the inbound lead from whichever source sent it.

Works with:
  Fluent Forms — flat JSON or nested 'names' object
  CallRail — detected by presence of caller_number or tracking_number
  Generic form POST — any JSON or form-encoded payload with standard field names

is_valid_lead is false when: (1) the payload has no name, email, or phone, or (2) both the name AND email contain 'test' (developer test submission).

To capture UTM parameters and gclid from ad clicks: add hidden fields named utm_source, utm_medium, utm_campaign, and gclid to every Fluent Form placed on a page with UTM parameters in the URL. Google Ads fills gclid automatically when auto-tagging is enabled in your Google Ads account.

Output: lead_name, lead_email, lead_phone, matter_type, message, raw_source, source_detail, form_source, referrer, utm_source, utm_medium, utm_campaign, gclid, received_at, is_valid_lead.

nodes.name: If Valid Lead
nodes.notes:
Filters out test submissions and payloads with no contact information before any CRM or sheet writes.

Yes → proceed to source tagging and CRM creation.
No → stop here. Nothing is written to Salesmate or Google Sheets.

nodes.name: Ignore Invalid Lead
nodes.notes: Stops the workflow for developer test submissions and payloads with no identifiable contact. Visible in execution history so you can confirm the filter is working.

nodes.name: Tag Lead Source
nodes.notes:
Determines which channel brought this lead and assigns a canonical name for the dashboard.

Channel mapping (first match wins, applied to combined utm_source + utm_medium + referrer + raw_source):
  google-ads — gclid present (Google Ads auto-tag) or signals match 'cpc', 'ppc', 'google ads'
  social-media — facebook, instagram, meta
  referral — 'referral', 'word of mouth', 'attorney', 'colleague', 'friend', 'family'
  legal-directory — Avvo, Justia, FindLaw, Martindale, Nolo, 'directory'
  yelp — yelp
  email-campaign — email, newsletter, campaign, drip
  inbound-call — payload came from a CallRail webhook
  website-organic — organic, seo, blog, or any referrer URL present
  unknown — no recognisable signal

To improve accuracy: add hidden fields utm_source, utm_medium, utm_campaign, gclid to every Fluent Form on a page where UTM parameters may be present. Enable auto-tagging in Google Ads to fill gclid on every ad click.

Also reads firm config from n8n Variables and builds the acknowledgment message text.

nodes.name: Add Lead to Salesmate
nodes.notes:
Creates the contact in Salesmate and tags them with their source channel — so every lead in your CRM shows exactly where they came from.

Tags written (comma-separated CSV): 'lead-source-google-ads', 'matter-family-law', etc. Filter contacts by these tags in Salesmate to see all leads from a specific channel.
Description field records source channel, source detail, received timestamp, and first message.

Note: this step does not deduplicate. If the same person submits a form twice, two Salesmate contacts are created. The done-for-you upgrade includes deduplication by email.

If this step fails (e.g., wrong API token), the workflow continues — the lead is still logged to Google Sheets and the firm notification fires.

Credential to wire: project → Credentials tab → New → Header Auth
  Header Name: accessToken
  Header Value: your Salesmate Personal API Token
  Find it: Salesmate → Profile icon (top right) → My Account → Developer → Personal API Token

nodes.name: Read Salesmate Response
Log Lead to Dashboard
nodes.notes:
Appends a row to your Lead Log — the foundation of the ROI dashboard.

Columns written automatically by this workflow:
  date, channel, source_detail, lead_name, lead_email, lead_phone, matter_type, message preview, Salesmate contact ID

Columns you fill manually in the sheet to complete the ROI calculation:
  ad_spend — what you spent on this channel this period (enter once per channel, not per row)
  signed_case — yes/no when the lead becomes a client
  case_value — the retainer or total fee when a case signs

Once the manual columns have data, use Insert → Pivot Table to see cost-per-lead and cost-per-signed-case by channel. This is the free-template version of the ROI dashboard.

Credential: project → Credentials tab → New → Google Sheets OAuth2 → sign in with the account that owns this sheet.

nodes.name: If Has Email Address
nodes.notes:
Decides whether to send an acknowledgment email to the prospect.

Yes (email present) → compliance check, then acknowledgment.
No (no email — e.g., a CallRail call where no email was captured) → skip acknowledgment and go straight to the firm alert.

This gate also prevents the Bar-Compliance Guardrail from throwing a 'missing recipient' error on a blank email address.

nodes.name: Compliance Check — Acknowledgment
nodes.notes:
Calls the Bar-Compliance Guardrail before sending the acknowledgment email to the prospect.

Checks whether this contact has opted out, appends the required unsubscribe footer to the message, and logs the attempt to your compliance audit sheet.

Returns { approved, message_out, recipient, suppression_reason } for the next step to act on.

Set GUARDRAIL_WORKFLOW_ID in n8n Variables to the numeric workflow ID from the Bar-Compliance Guardrail URL: .../workflow/WORKFLOW_ID.

Prerequisite: the Bar-Compliance Guardrail must be deployed, the OPS1_SHEET_ID Variable must be set, and the guardrail must be active.

nodes.name: If Approved to Acknowledge
nodes.notes:
Routes based on the compliance guardrail's decision.

Yes (approved: true) → sends the acknowledgment email to the prospect.
No (approved: false) → contact has opted out; message suppressed. The guardrail has already logged this suppression to the compliance audit sheet.

nodes.name: Send Acknowledgment to Lead
nodes.notes:
Sends the prospect a brief acknowledgment confirming their inquiry was received.

The message body comes from the compliance guardrail's message_out — it has already had the required unsubscribe footer appended.

Credential: project → Credentials tab → New → SMTP → enter your firm email settings.
  Gmail: Settings → Security → App Passwords (not your regular Gmail password)
  Outlook 365: email + password; enable SMTP AUTH in Microsoft 365 admin centre
  Other providers: check your provider's SMTP settings page for host, port, and credentials

nodes.name: Skip — contact opted out
nodes.notes: No acknowledgment sent — contact is on the opt-out list. The compliance guardrail has already logged this suppression to the audit sheet. The lead is still in your Google Sheets dashboard and Salesmate. The firm notification fires next.

nodes.name: Build Firm Alert
nodes.notes:
Assembles the firm alert email content from earlier nodes — runs regardless of which path the workflow took.

Receives from three possible paths:
  Acknowledgment sent — $json.approved = true
  Acknowledgment suppressed — $json.approved = false (opted out)
  No email address — $json.approved is undefined (skipped the compliance check)

All three paths produce the same firm notification. The ack_status field reflects which path was taken.

nodes.name: Notify Firm of New Lead
nodes.notes:
Emails your firm a summary of every new lead so the intake team can follow up immediately.

Includes: name, email, phone, matter type, message, source channel, source detail, a direct link to the Salesmate contact, and whether an acknowledgment was sent.

The subject line includes the channel ('New google-ads lead: Jane Smith') so you can filter by channel in your inbox.

Credential: same SMTP account used for the acknowledgment email.

parameters.subject: =New {{ $json.channel }} lead: {{ $json.lead_name }}

body.message: I need help with a divorce filing. My spouse and I agreed to separate and I want to make sure the process is done right.

description: Tags every inbound lead with its source channel (web form, call, referral, ad, etc.), creates the contact in Salesmate CRM, and appends a row to your Google Sheets ROI dashboard — giving you a live view of cost-per-signed-case by channel. Replaces CallRail Analytics ($45–145/mo) + manual spreadsheets (~3 hrs/week).
